LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT STEVEN BRAGG'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE AND VACATE DEFAULT JUDGMENT AS VOID PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 473(D) - DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO VACATE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS DENIED. ALTHOUGH DEFENDANT CLEARLY IS SUFFERING FROM A SERIOUS DISABLING CONDITION, EVEN GIVEN DEFENDANT'S APPARENTLY REDUCED CAPACITY, HE DID NOT ACT WITH REASONABLE DILIGENCE IN SEEKING TO SET ASIDE THE JUDGMENT WITHIN A REASONABLE TIME. APPLICATION PENDING FOR WAIVER OF COURT FEES AND COSTS PURSUANT TO G.C. 68633, CRC 3.51, 8.26, AND 8.818
Off Calendar Dec-21-2011 Denied

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Oct-11-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT STEVEN BRAGG'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE AND VACATE DEFAULT JUDGMENT AS VOID PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 473(d).

APPEARANCES: Elizabeth MacDonald, Esq. 321-9025 for pltf & opposing party; Otis C. Landerholm, Esq. of Landerholm Law Office 322-0960 for deft & moving party.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Defendant's motion to vacate default and default judgment is denied. Although defendant clearly is suffering from a serious disabling condition, even given defendant's apparently reduced capacity, he did not act with reasonable diligence in seeking to set aside the judgment within a reasonable time. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Sep-29-2011 Continued To Oct-11-11 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
DEFENDANT STEVEN BRAGG'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND IS CONTINUED TO OCTOBER 11, 2011 FOR DEFENDANT TO PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302 NO LATER THAN 10/3/11. SEE LOCAL RULE 2.6 (B). Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR # 8098 =301/EHG
